title: The Walk
content:
You did not walk with me
        Of late to the hill-top tree
         By the gated ways,
         As in earlier days;
         You were weak and lame,
         So you never came,
    And I went alone, and I did not mind,
    Not thinking of you as left behind.

        I walked up there to-day
        Just in the former way:
         Surveyed around
         The familiar ground
         By myself again:
         What difference, then?
    Only that underlying sense
    Of the look of a room on returning thence.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Hardy, titled "The Walk", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
